rationale: Confirmed by diff (git diff 62cd6df3..2b55d1a7 -- internal/validate/spec.go) that the delta is exactly the regex literal + its comment + 3 new subtests; zero AddError/AddWarning call sites touched, so my round-1 severity-pin finding stands unchanged and unaffected. Probed the new pattern `\[(ADR-\d{4})([^0-9\]][^\]]*)?\]\([^)]+\)` empirically (scratch Go program, not the shared worktree): `[ADR-0031]` (group 2 absent) and `[ADR-0031-...]` (single-char/filename tail) both still match with group 1 = 'ADR-0031' as before; `[ADR-12345]`, `[ADR-00311]`, and `[ADR-003100]` now produce ZERO matches (no truncation, no false-pass-via-prefix) — the digit-boundary fix works exactly as the commit message and new tests claim, confirmed independent of trusting the tests. Verified group-2-unused submatch-indexing safety directly: FindStringSubmatch on a group-2-absent input returns a length-3 slice (`["[ADR-0031](x)" "ADR-0031" ""]`) — Go's regexp submatch slices are always fixed-length with unmatched optional groups as empty string, never a shorter slice, so `id := m[1]` (spec.go, unchanged) is safe regardless of whether group 2 participates. Ran the full internal/validate suite plus go build on 2b55d1a7 in the fbel.2 worktree: all PASS, git status clean throughout. My round-1 low-severity nested-bracket finding is unaffected (still no-match, unaddressed by this fix, and out of this fix's stated scope).
findings: Confirmed fixed (empirically): 5-digit IDs (ADR-12345) and 4-digit-prefix-plus-trailing-digit IDs (ADR-00311, ADR-003100) now produce zero regex matches instead of truncating/false-passing via a shorter valid prefix.

Confirmed safe: group 2 being optional does not create an indexing hazard — Go's FindAllStringSubmatch always returns a full-width slice per match (unmatched optional groups are "", never omitted), so the unchanged `id := m[1]` extraction line is unaffected by the new optional group.

Unchanged from round 1 (not a regression, out of this fix's scope): a touchpoint link with a nested `]` in its text (e.g. `[ADR-0031 [foo]](url)`) still produces zero matches — still a silent false-negative for that contrived authoring shape, still non-blocking.
